channel.title: Marie Rimmer
channel.description: Member of Parliament for St Helens South and Whiston

item.title: Pride in St Helens Newsletter | Issue 1
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
The first issue of the Pride in Place Newsletter includes a visit from Steve Reed MP and an update on the Council's role in the process.

The post Pride in St Helens Newsletter | Issue 1 appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
It’s a pleasure to welcome you to the Pride in St Helens newsletter, thank you for signing up to be part of this exciting journey. Pride in Place is about putting communities at the heart of regeneration, and that means having residents involved every step of the way, shaping priorities, sharing ideas, and helping to build a stronger future for our neighbourhoods. Your voice is vital to making this a success.

 

If you know others who would like to stay informed and get involved, please do share the sign-up form and help us grow this community for the years to come.

Rt Hon Steve Reed joins Pride in St Helens

On Friday 24th April, Steve Reed MP, Government Minister for Housing, Communities and Local Government visited the borough, joining Marie Rimmer MP, Steve Rotheram, David Baines MP and Councillor Anthony Burns in touring the town centre and fingerpost for Pride in St Helens.

 

The visit provided an opportunity to showcase the significant regeneration currently taking place in St Helens town centre. These developments highlight the impact of strong collaboration between local, regional and national government, with partners working together to drive investment, improve infrastructure and create new opportunities for residents and businesses.

 

As part of the programme, the group visited Fingerpost, where they canvassed and talked with local residents to hear first-hand about their ideas for the Pride in St Helens initiative.

 

The visit also highlighted the wider Pride in Place agenda being delivered nationally, with Steve Reed MP playing a key role in advancing this work.

 

Overall, the visit underlined the importance of sustained partnership working and community engagement in delivering meaningful change, with St Helens continuing to position itself as a leading example of how targeted regeneration funding can make a lasting difference.

St Helens Council’s Cabinet discusses Pride in St Helens

On 22nd April, St Helens Council Cabinet discussed the next phase of the Pride in Place programme, as part of the Government’s Tranche Two investment.

 

The report set out a series of key considerations for Cabinet, most notably the opportunity for the borough to formally accept Pride in Place status for these areas.

 

This designation brings with it a long-term commitment to regeneration, backed by up to £20 million of funding over the next decade, aimed at revitalising neighbourhoods and supporting communities identified as facing significant challenges.

 

A central recommendation was the establishment of a dedicated Pride in Place Board. This board will be responsible for shaping a 10-year vision for the area, alongside a detailed three-year investment plan. The approach reflects a shift towards more locally driven regeneration, placing residents, businesses and community organisations at the heart of decision-making. Cabinet also acknowledged the role of Marie Rimmer MP in her responsibility for selecting the independent Chair of the Board, recognising the importance of strong leadership in driving this work forward.

 

Cabinet also considered the governance arrangements required to support the programme. This includes a proposal for the Council to act as the accountable body for the funding, ensuring that investment is delivered transparently and in line with public finance and procurement regulations. In addition, a process will begin to recruit an independent Chair to lead the new board, with a strong emphasis on community representation and inclusive participation.

 

Early-stage funding of £150,000 has already been allocated to support the development of the board and the initial planning process. This will enable the Council to begin community engagement activity, recruit key personnel, and lay the groundwork for the long-term regeneration plan.

 

The report also highlighted clear timelines for delivery. The Pride in Place Board is expected to be established by summer 2026, with a full plan submitted by the end of November. Subject to approval, the first major tranche of funding for projects is anticipated from April 2027.

 

You can view the discussion here.

The post Pride in St Helens Newsletter | Issue 1 appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie hosts public forum on Pride in St Helens funding
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 26th March, Marie hosted a community event for residents to share their thoughts on how £20 million in funding would be spent in their neighbourhood.

The post Marie hosts public forum on Pride in St Helens funding appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
On 26th March, Marie hosted a community discussion with local residents to hear their early thoughts on how Pride in St Helens can be shaped over the next decade.

The session brought together voices from across the borough, giving people the opportunity to share ideas, priorities and ambitions for the future of the town. Marie emphasised the importance of keeping residents informed and involved at every stage, making clear that this will be a community-led process.

Over the coming days and weeks, further opportunities will be made available for people to have their say, ensuring that as many voices as possible help shape the vision going forward.

Marie highlighted that this work is about coming together as a community and rebuilding a shared sense of pride in St Helens.

She also extended her thanks to St Helens Parish Church for once again being such welcoming and supportive hosts.

The post Marie hosts public forum on Pride in St Helens funding appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie speaks on type 1 diabetes diagnosis
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 11th March, Marie spoke in Westminister Hall on the importance of early testing for Type 1 diabetes in children.

The post Marie speaks on type 1 diabetes diagnosis appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
On 10th March, Marie spoke in Westminster Hall about the importance of early testing for Type 1 diabetes in children.

She shared the story of Gracie, a young girl from Newton-le-Willows, who was rushed to hospital at just one year old with undiagnosed Type 1 diabetes. Doctors later told her parents she had been only minutes away from losing her life.

Thankfully Gracie survived and is now thriving, but too many families are not so fortunate. Early detection can save lives, prevent trauma for families, and ensure children receive the treatment they need sooner.

Marie stated “We must continue working towards a future where routine testing helps identify Type 1 diabetes earlier and protects more children and families.

I would like to thank not only Gracie’s parents for sharing their story with me, but with the hundreds in our constituency who signed the petition to get this to Parliament for debate.”

View the full video here: https://www.facebook.com/share/v/1GmGzAUSJk/

The post Marie speaks on type 1 diabetes diagnosis appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie writes to Government on coalfield regeneration
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 5th March, Marie wrote to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury, urging the Government to support new investment for coalfield communities through the Coalfields Regeneration Trust.  The Trust is seeking £50 million of government investment, which it will match to create a £100 million fund to build industrial units, support small businesses and create […]

The post Marie writes to Government on coalfield regeneration appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
On 5th March, Marie wrote to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury, urging the Government to support new investment for coalfield communities through the Coalfields Regeneration Trust.

 
The Trust is seeking £50 million of government investment, which it will match to create a £100 million fund to build industrial units, support small businesses and create jobs in former mining communities.
 
Coalfield communities still face significant challenges, and this investment could help drive growth while supporting local organisations doing vital work.

The post Marie writes to Government on coalfield regeneration appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie speaks on palliative and end-of-life care
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
Today, Marie spoke in the House about the importance of proper funding and a national strategy for palliative and end-of-life care. She shared her own family’s experience and highlighted the incredible work of Willowbrook Hospice, which has provided compassionate care to thousands of people in our community. Hospices play a vital role in ensuring people can spend […]

The post Marie speaks on palliative and end-of-life care appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
Today, Marie spoke in the House about the importance of proper funding and a national strategy for palliative and end-of-life care.
 
She shared her own family’s experience and highlighted the incredible work of Willowbrook Hospice, which has provided compassionate care to thousands of people in our community.
 
Hospices play a vital role in ensuring people can spend the end of their lives with dignity and compassion, but many are under severe financial pressure. We must ensure they receive the support they need to continue this essential work.
 
Full video available here: https://www.facebook.com/share/v/1BntKJBpcW/

The post Marie speaks on palliative and end-of-life care appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Pride in St Helens is coming!
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
After years of being overlooked and underfunded, several neighbourhoods in our borough have been chosen for significant Government investment — and this time, it’s residents who will decide how it’s spent. Through the Pride in Place Programme, £20 million of long-term funding will be invested across the following areas: – Town Centre East- Fingerpost- Sutton North West- […]

The post Pride in St Helens is coming! appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
After years of being overlooked and underfunded, several neighbourhoods in our borough have been chosen for significant Government investment — and this time, it’s residents who will decide how it’s spent.
 
Through the Pride in Place Programme, £20 million of long-term funding will be invested across the following areas:
 
– Town Centre East
– Fingerpost
– Sutton North West
– Merton Bank
– Pocket Nook
– Peasley Cross
– Parr Stocks
 
This is funding that can make a real, practical difference. It could support local jobs and opportunities, improve community facilities and youth services, enhance public spaces, make our streets safer, cleaner and more welcoming, and strengthen the local organisations that bring people together.
 
In the coming weeks, we’ll be sharing details on how you can have your say and shape the improvements you want to see.
 
This is a genuine opportunity to build a better, more prideful St Helens — together.

The post Pride in St Helens is coming! appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie write to Government on the West Bank
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 3rd March, Marie joined colleagues in writing to the foreign secretary to raise concerns around the situation in the West Bank

The post Marie write to Government on the West Bank appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
On 3rd March, Marie joined colleagues from across Parliament in writing to the Foreign Secretary about the escalating situation in the West Bank.

They raised serious concerns about the expansion of illegal settlements, land confiscation, and the increasing displacement of Palestinian communities.
 
Marie stated: “I urge the Government to move beyond statements of condemnation and consider specific measures to uphold international law and protect the prospects of a viable Palestinian state.”

The post Marie write to Government on the West Bank appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie Tackles the Imact of JLR Cyber Attack on Local Business
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 18th September, Marie signed a letter to Government and called for local business to contact in on the impact that the Jaguar Land Rover cyber attack had.

The post Marie Tackles the Imact of JLR Cyber Attack on Local Business appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
“This morning, I joined colleagues from Merseyside and the West Midlands in writing to the Secretary of State for Business, urging government to support workers and businesses in the Jaguar Land Rover supply chain during the shutdown.”

“If your business has been affected by the Jaguar Land Rover cyber attack, please email marie.rimmer.mp@parliament.uk with:
•⁠ ⁠How this has impacted you
•⁠ ⁠What support you need

Be sure to include your full postal address when contacting.

You can check if I am your MP here: https://members.parliament.uk/FindYourMP. I can only act on behalf of constituents living in my area.”

The post Marie Tackles the Imact of JLR Cyber Attack on Local Business appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie Welcomes Hillsborough Law’s Introduction to Parliament.
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 16th September, Marie released a statement on the news that the Hillsborough Law would be introduced to Parliament.

The post Marie Welcomes Hillsborough Law’s Introduction to Parliament. appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
“I welcome the Government’s decision to bring the Hillsborough Law before Parliament today.

This is a long-awaited moment, and I am deeply grateful that what campaigners and families have fought for, for decades, is finally becoming law.

I have been proud to back the push for this in recent months alongside colleagues. This law is a large and necessary step towards delivering justice for the victims and the families. It will help ensure truth, transparency and accountability where too often those things were denied. I would like to echo Mayor Steve Rotheram‘s words that this can be a lasting legacy to the 97.

But we must be clear now as we were before, this legislation must be protected as it passes through Parliament so it can do the job intended, we will not accept a watered-down version.

Finally, thank you to all those who have campaigned so tirelessly, especially the families whose courage has driven this entire campaign, to Mayor Andy Burnham for starting this campaign, and finally to Ian Byrne MP for his leadership in recent months. Their determination has brought us to this landmark moment.”

The post Marie Welcomes Hillsborough Law’s Introduction to Parliament. appeared first on Marie Rimmer.

item.title: Marie asks parents for their SEND experiences.
item.dc:creator: OfficeTeam2
item.description:
On 15th September, Marie made a public request for parents to share their SEND stories, so that she can take these forward in meetings with local and Government representative.

The post Marie asks parents for their SEND experiences. appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
item.content:encoded:
In recent weeks, I have met with both local and Government representatives to share the real experiences of constituents who have contacted me about SEND.

We all want the very best for our children with special educational needs. That is why I am asking you to write to me and share your own experiences. Your voice is important, and I will continue to use them to push for the changes we need in future discussions about improving the system.

You can email me at marie.rimmer.mp@parliament.uk, please remember to include your full postal address when getting in touch.

If you would prefer not to use email, details of my next surgery, dedicated to SEND issues, will be shared in the coming days.

The post Marie asks parents for their SEND experiences. appeared first on Marie Rimmer.
